status: final (with overnight corrections appended 2026-07-02 by AGENT-171)
purpose: Internal comparison of 1D loop model forms F0-F5: what each assumes, how it is driven, its error vs CFD (mdot and per-segment dP), and the theory for why it errs.

headline_findings.conclusion: mdot is NOT a robust closure-quality metric; condition-dependent. Per-segment dP distribution is robust.
headline_findings.open_discrepancy: Factor-2 mdot gap at matched-T with CFD friction — no validated mechanism yet
headline_findings.dominant_lever: Thermal boundary (effective insulation) >> hydraulic closures for mdot

limitations: All CFD reference is coarse mesh (no GCI bound, T6 blocked).
1D 'matched insulation' = matched loop T / effective UA, not identical BC form to CFD.
Overnight corrections (section 6) appended 2026-07-02 — read those before citing section 5.
